content.problemStatement:
If you travel to work by taxi, it costs you XXX dollars. If you travel by foot it is, of course,
free of charge. You want to minimize the cost of travel, but you don’t want to walk
when it’s raining or it has rained the day before. Given the weather forecast for the
next NNN days (111 if the rain is falling, 000 otherwise), calculate the cost of travel.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains 222 integers NNN - number of days and XXX - cost for taxi
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers D1,D2…DnD_1, D_2 … D_nD1​,D2​…Dn​


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing one integer - Total cost of the travel.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 100000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and X — the number of days and the taxi cost per day. The second line contains N space‑separated integers D1 … DN, where Di is 1 if it rains on day i and 0 otherwise.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total minimum cost of travelling to work over the N days.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: No rain on the only day, so you can walk → cost 0.\nTest case 2: Day 1 rains → taxi (cost 47). Day 2 is dry but yesterday rained → taxi again (another 47). Total = 94.

solution.approach: Traverse the days of a test case from left to right. Keep a flag indicating whether it rained on the previous day. For the current day i, a taxi is required if Di == 1 (it rains today) or if the previous‑day flag is true (it rained yesterday). Whenever a taxi is needed, add X to the answer. After processing the day, update the previous‑day flag to Di. This yields the minimum possible cost because walking is allowed only when today and yesterday are both rain‑free.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of days. Over all test cases the total is O(ΣN) ≤ 10^5.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input storage.
